retry_reason: The queue may not exist in the hash; retrying without any change is pointless. Caller should handle NULL appropriately.
contract_summary: Get-wrapper that finds an ap_queue by qid in a global hash table and increments the reference count on its embedded device via get_device. The caller receives the pointer with an elevated reference and must call put_device on &aq->ap_dev.device when done. The function holds a spinlock during the lookup but releases it before returning. Returns NULL if no matching queue exists; callers must check the return value.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to look up an ap_queue by qid and acquire a reference on its device via get_device. The hash iteration is the necessary mechanism to find the object, not incidental business logic. The only refcount-relevant operation is get_device.

check.reason: The extracted operation is consistent: get_device(&aq->ap_dev.device) increments refcount on the device embedded in the returned ap_queue object. The access path starts from the return value (aq) and correctly traces through ap_dev, device, kobj, kref to refcount_t. Segment count (6) matches type chain count (6).
